what: Update M<=32 K>1024 config from legacy BSM=32/BSN=64/BSK=512/NW=8/NS=1 to BSM=8/BSN=128/BSK=256/NW=4/NS=2/.cg — match proven K<=1024 config for better CU utilization (224 vs 112 blocks) and pipelining
why: 32x7168x2048 and 32xNx4096 shapes use legacy config from v21 era. BSM=8/BSN=128 was proven optimal for M=32 K<=1024 in v099. Legacy config has only 0.44 waves (112 blocks), no pipelining. Updated config doubles grid to 0.875 waves and enables NS=2 software pipelining. v132 tried similar but misidentified it as not hitting any LB shapes.

what: Lower FUSED_M_THRESHOLD from 64 to 32 — route M=64 (64x7168x2048) to two-phase (quant + ASM GEMM) instead of fused Triton preshuffle. ASM GEMM tuned at 6.81us + quant ~3us = ~10us vs fused 13.3us BM
why: Tuned CSV shows ASM 32x128 GEMM takes only 6.81us for 64x7168x2048. Fused preshuffle kernel takes 13.3us BM. Two-phase should be ~10us total. The dead branch 'Triton fused GEMM for M>=128' only covers M>=128; M=64 two-phase was tried early (v31-v43) before fused was tuned, but never re-evaluated after ASM path was optimized with direct dispatch.

what_failed: Two-phase (quant+ASM) for M=64 slower than fused: 14.4us vs 13.7us LB. Two kernel launches (quant ~3us + ASM ~7us) have more overhead than single fused preshuffle kernel.
